T0016.title: SIM Bank
T0016.definition: A modem pool, a telephone-based expansion device that can accept multiple simultaneous dial-up connections. When fitted with SIM cards, it can send and receive multiple SMS messages or make calls concurrently. Historically used by institutions like post offices, customs, banks, and call centers for remote networking, it has been repurposed by grey-market actors as a tool for bulk account registration, reselling access, or conducting fraud attacks against internet platforms through mass 'spray' techniques.
T0016.description: A 'modem pool card' refers to a physical SIM card held by a card dealer, typically inserted into a modem pool device for receiving SMS verification codes. Dealers source these cards primarily from major carriers, virtual network operators, and IoT card channels.
